in entire doctor, with detection and pointers to run doctor

metadata_reconcile.go:128
Nit: ReconcileDisconnectedMetadataBranch creates its own context.Background() here, but callers like getBranchCheckpoints already have a ctx. Threading context through would allow cancellation to propagate and would be more idiomatic Go.

# Manual Test: Disconnected Metadata Branch Reconciliation

Binary: `/tmp/entire-test`
Test repo: `entirehq/terrible-temp-test-repo-that-sucks-to-test-pr-533`

## Setup

```bash
# Clean slate
cd /tmp
rm -rf terrible-temp-test-repo-that-sucks-to-test-pr-533
git clone git@github.com:entirehq/terrible-temp-test-repo-that-sucks-to-test-pr-533.git
cd terrible-temp-test-repo-that-sucks-to-test-pr-533

# Fetch the remote metadata branch (creates remote-tracking ref)
git fetch origin entire/checkpoints/v1

# Create local metadata branch from remote (normal state)
git branch entire/checkpoints/v1 origin/entire/checkpoints/v1

# Verify: local and remote should match
git log --oneline entire/checkpoints/v1 -3
git log --oneline origin/entire/checkpoints/v1 -3
```

## Test 1: Doctor reports OK when branches are connected

```bash
/tmp/entire-test doctor --force
```

**Expected:** `Metadata branches: OK` followed by stuck sessions check.

**Actual**
```
Metadata branches: OK

No stuck sessions found.
```

---

## Test 2: Break it - create a disconnected local metadata branch

```bash
# Create a disconnected orphan branch with a fake local checkpoint
git checkout --orphan temp-orphan
git rm -rf .
mkdir -p ff/ff00112233
echo '{"checkpoint_id":"ffff00112233","session_id":"test-disconnected"}' > ff/ff00112233/metadata.json
git add .
git commit -m "Checkpoint: ffff00112233"

# Point local metadata branch to this disconnected orphan
git branch -f entire/checkpoints/v1 temp-orphan
git checkout main

# Verify they're disconnected (exit code 1 = no common ancestor)
git merge-base entire/checkpoints/v1 origin/entire/checkpoints/v1
echo "Exit code: $?"
# Should print "Exit code: 1"
```
**Expected:** `Exit code: 1`
**Actual** `Exit code: 1`

---

```bash
# explain reads checkpoints - should show the disconnected

## Test 3: Warning on read paths (explain)

```bash
# explain reads checkpoints - should show the disconnected warning
/tmp/entire-test explain 2>&1
```

**Expected:** Stderr shows:
```
[entire] Warning: Local and remote session metadata branches are disconnected.
[entire] Some checkpoints from remote may not be visible. Run 'entire doctor' to fix.
```

The warning should appear only once even if multiple read paths are hit.

**Actual**
```
[entire] Warning: Local and remote session metadata branches are disconnected.
[entire] Some checkpoints from remote may not be visible. Run 'entire doctor' to fix.
Branch: main
Checkpoints: 0

No checkpoints found on this branch.
Checkpoints will appear here after you save changes during a Claude session.
```
---

## Test 4: Doctor detects and explains the problem

```bash
/tmp/entire-test doctor
```

**Expected output:**
```
Metadata branches: DISCONNECTED
  Local and remote entire/checkpoints/v1 branches share no common ancestor.
  Some remote checkpoints may not be visible locally.
  Fix: cherry-pick local checkpoints onto remote tip (preserves all data).
? Fix disconnected metadata branches? Yes
```

**Say Yes.** Then expect:
```
[entire] Detected disconnected session metadata (local and remote share no common ancestor)
[entire] Cherry-picking 1 local checkpoint(s) onto remote...
[entire] Done — all local and remote checkpoints preserved
  -> Fixed: metadata branches reconciled
```

**Actual**
```
Metadata branches: DISCONNECTED
  Local and remote entire/checkpoints/v1 branches share no common ancestor.
  Some remote checkpoints may not be visible locally.
  Fix: cherry-pick local checkpoints onto remote tip (preserves all data).

  YES


[entire] Detected disconnected session metadata (local and remote share no common ancestor)
[entire] Cherry-picking 1 local checkpoint(s) onto remote...
[entire] Done — all local and remote checkpoints preserved
  -> Fixed: metadata branches reconciled

No stuck sessions found.
```
---

## Test 8: Empty orphan case (no local data, just the bug artifact)

```bash
# Create empty orphan (the exact bug scenario)
git checkout --orphan temp-orphan-4
git rm -rf .
git commit --allow-empty -m "Initialize metadata branch"
git branch -f entire/checkpoints/v1 temp-orphan-4
git checkout main

# Doctor should detect and fix by just resetting to remote
/tmp/entire-test doctor --force
```

**Expected:**
```
Metadata branches: DISCONNECTED
  ...
[entire] Detected disconnected session metadata (local and remote share no common ancestor)
[entire] Done — local had no checkpoint data, reset to remote
  -> Fixed: metadata branches reconciled
```

**Actual**
```
Metadata branches: DISCONNECTED
  Local and remote entire/checkpoints/v1 branches share no common ancestor.
  Some remote checkpoints may not be visible locally.
  Fix: cherry-pick local checkpoints onto remote tip (preserves all data).
[entire] Detected disconnected session metadata (local and remote share no common ancestor)
[entire] Done — local had no checkpoint data, reset to remote
  -> Fixed: metadata branches reconciled

No stuck sessions found.
```

```bash
# Verify local now matches remote exactly
git rev-parse entire/checkpoints/v1
git rev-parse origin/entire/checkpoints/v1
# Should be the same hash
```

**Actual**
```
20:38 /tmp/terrible-temp-test-repo-that-sucks-to-test-pr-533 main $ git rev-parse entire/checkpoints/v1
32d64a5e58fe53b99d37f696f3721eb7e3d4aa21
20:38 /tmp/terrible-temp-test-repo-that-sucks-to-test-pr-533 main $ git rev-parse origin/entire/checkpoints/v1
32d64a5e58fe53b99d37f696f3721eb7e3d4aa21
```
